channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Application of plant viruses in biotechnology with relation to artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology
item.description: This review examines the application of plant viruses in biotechnology, with emphasis on their integration with artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology approaches. The article explores how these emerging technologies can enhance the development and utilization of plant virus-ba

item.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
item.description: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

item.title: STAT+: The quest to save Grace — and clear the way for rare disease patients everywhere
item.description: How a father’s quest may shape the future of rare disease gene therapy.

item.title: Alchemab lands £25m from british business bank in record life sciences bet
item.description: Cambridge biotech Alchemab Therapeutics has extended its Series A round with a £25 million ($34 million) investment from the British Business Bank, which the company says is the state-owned lender’s largest backing of a life sciences company to date. The mone…

item.title: Biological aging clocks in health and disease
item.description: This review outlines the state of the art of biological clocks and how they could help understand pathobiology, reduce the burden of disease and extend healthspan.

item.title: Courts split over AI training fair use rulings [let's data science]
item.description: U.S. courts remain split on whether training AI models on copyrighted material is fair use, after **Judge William Alsup** ruled in June 2025 that training on purchased, digitized books was fair use while **Judge Vince Chhabria** reached the opposite conclusio…

item.title: Colorado’s admt comment window closes with no industry voice arguing for agent governance
item.description: Colorado’s recent pre-rulemaking comment period for the ADMT Act (SB 26-189) was defined by a conspicuous void. Between June 15 and July 13, 2026, stakeholders had a rare window to shape the regulatory future of automated decision-making. Yet, across the subm…

item.title: Human embryonic stem cell-derived dopaminergic cells for parkinson’s disease: a phase 1/2 open-label trial
item.description: A phase 1/2 trial of an off-the-shelf stem cell-derived dopaminergic progenitor therapy demonstrated a favorable 12-month safety profile, with no graft-related adverse events, dyskinesias or tumor formation, although risks were associated with the immunosuppr…

item.title: 85% of financial firms plan AI budget increases
item.description: Enterprise artificial intelligence (AI) is no longer just a technology project. It is becoming a line item with momentum. That’s the clearest signal from “The Enterprise AI Benchmark Report: Financial Services Pulls Ahead in the Enterprise AI Race,” a new PYM…

item.title: When the real world becomes the trial
item.description: Once confined to post-market surveillance, real-world evidence is now being used to emulate trials, guide approvals and even run studies in real time, forcing a rethink of what constitutes a clinical trial.

item.title: Anthropic's new "j-lens" reveals a silent workspace inside claude that mirrors a leading theory of consciousness | venturebeat
item.description: Anthropic’s new Claude research reveals a hidden internal “global workspace” that resembles human conscious processing, raising major questions about AI reasoning, interpretability, safety, and machine consciousness.

item.title: Molecular design beyond training data with novel extended objective functionals of generative AI models driven by quantum annealing computer
item.description: This study presents a novel approach to molecular design that extends generative AI model capabilities beyond their training data through the use of extended objective functionals combined with quantum annealing computing. The method integrates quantum annealing technology to optimize molecular stru

item.title: Proteome-scale quantification of the interactions driving condensate formation of intrinsically disordered proteins
item.description: This study quantifies protein-protein interactions at the proteome scale that drive condensate formation by intrinsically disordered proteins, providing systematic characterization of the molecular mechanisms underlying phase separation. The research integrates experimental and computational approac

item.title: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) in neuropsychiatric drug discovery: current challenges and future directions
item.description: This review examines the application of artificial intelligence in neuropsychiatric drug discovery, identifying current technical and practical obstacles while proposing strategies to advance the field. The authors discuss how AI can address existing limitations in the drug development process for p

item.title: STAT+: 931 days. The drug approval scandal hiding in plain sight
item.description: Two years, six months, and 18 days have elapsed since Northwest Biotherapeutics submitted its brain cancer treatment for regulatory approval.

item.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s no. 2 role
item.description: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.

item.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
item.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

item.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
item.description: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

item.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
item.description: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

item.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
item.description: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

item.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
item.description: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

item.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
item.description: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.

item.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
item.description: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

item.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
item.description: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the

item.title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
item.description: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.

item.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
item.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

item.title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
item.description: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."

item.title: 3br-mgd: few-shot toxicity prediction with a three-branch deep encoder and meta-learning framework
item.description: Scientific Reports - 3Br-MGD: few-shot toxicity prediction with a three-branch deep encoder and meta-learning framework

item.title: Openai secures u.s. regulatory green light for gpt-5.6 rollout, axios report says
item.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce has cleared the way for OpenAI to proceed with a broad release of its advanced GPT-5.6 model, Axios reported, citing a source familiar with the matter. OpenAI anticip… 1663

item.title: The future of governing AI agents
item.description: Autonomous security agents need governance built into the architecture across reasoning separation, continuous evaluation, progressive trust, and agent-specific telemetry at scale. ...

item.title: Healthcare interoperability apis: new marketing moments - ehealthcare solutions digital advertising network
item.description: Discover how healthcare interoperability APIs are transforming patient engagement, connected care, and healthcare marketing by creating smarter, more personalized digital experiences.

item.title: Nigeria must fund its health system, experts warns as donor support declines — expert
item.description: By Sola Ogundipe Health advocates have called on Nigeria to strengthen domestic financing for healthcare, warning that continued dependence on foreign donors could leave millions of Nigerians without access to lifesaving services if external funding continues…

item.title: Global breast cancer survival estimates in 2017–2021 to advance the WHO global breast cancer initiative
item.description: Benchmark estimates from 2017–2021 provide an updated assessment of progress toward the WHO Global Breast Cancer Initiative survival targets across all 194 Member States.

item.title: Oncology must confront hidden side effects
item.description: As a new generation of cancer drugs continues to extend the lives of people with cancer, strategies for recording and managing their toxicity and long-term effects will need innovation too.

item.title: Chromatin landscape and epigenetic heterogeneity of acute myeloid leukaemia
item.description: An ATAC-seq-based approach is used to classify acute myeloid leukaemia (AML) into 16 epigenomic subgroups, and provides insight into the role of non-genetic mechanisms in determining pathogenesis, clinical behaviour and drug sensitivity in this disease.

item.title: Diet–microbiome synergy underlies obesity-associated immunotherapy efficacy
item.description: Diet shapes obesity-associated therapeutic responses to immune checkpoint inhibitors through gut microbial metabolism and host anti-tumour immunity, demonstrated in mouse custom-diet models and human-to-mouse fecal microbiota transplantation ex…

item.title: This former deepmind exec thinks the AI arms race could end in disaster
item.description: Verity Harding tells WIRED that the US government’s nationalistic attitude toward AI is evidence that a worst-case scenario is taking shape.

item.title: AI is becoming a bargain hunter's market, with a few luxury models on top
item.description: Inference is become a commodity except for frontier models

item.title: Short- versus long-course of antimicrobial treatment in pediatric population with acute osteoarticular infections: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis compared short-course versus long-course antimicrobial treatment outcomes in pediatric patients with acute bone and joint infections. The study evaluates the clinical efficacy and safety profiles of different treatment duration strategies in this pediatric po

item.title: Smart/stimuli-responsive metal-organic frameworks for on-demand antibacterial therapy in the oral cavity
item.description: This study examines smart, stimuli-responsive metal-organic frameworks designed to deliver antibacterial agents on-demand in the oral cavity. These advanced materials respond to environmental triggers to release therapeutic compounds for treating oral infections. The research represents a novel appr

item.title: STAT+: Europe will accelerate its review of RevMed’s drug
item.description: Kailera's GLP-1 pill data raises concerns, FDA clears Vera autoimmune therapy, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a Vera kidney drug approval, a U.K. Enhertu pricing deal, and more
item.description: The FDA approved a kidney disease drug from Vera Therapeutics called Trutakna, to treat IgA nephropathy

item.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
item.description: Elon Musk's tech company released the newest version of Grok on Wednesday, promising a cheaper, more efficient alternative to other powerful AI models.

item.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
item.description: General Intuition is betting millions of hours of video game data can train the foundation models for physical AI, making it easier to build smarter robots with minimal real-world data.

item.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at understanding how things actually move through space and time&

item.title: Your gaming data could be the secret to AGI, according to this Bezos-backed startup
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at understanding how things actually move through space and time&

item.title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
item.description: Android Bench is evolving, and developers can help guide that process.

item.title: Powering scientific discovery: byokg and graphrag for intelligent pharmaceutical research
item.description: In this post, we explore how Graph-based Retrieval Augmented Generation (GraphRAG) is transforming scientific research by combining graph databases with generative AI. With this approach, you can accelerate discovery processes without compromising scientific …

item.title: Wall street is betting on eli lilly’s glp-1 portfolio — it’s missing the $1 trillion opportunity
item.description: The post Wall Street Is Betting on Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 Portfolio — It’s Missing the $1 Trillion Opportunity appeared first on 24/7 Wall St.. The pharmaceutical industry is entering a new era where biology and artificial intelligence are converging. Drug discove…

item.title: What will be the future of computational biology for macromolecules in the era of AI?
item.description: We have seen more progress in computational biology for macromolecules in the last five years than we experienced in the five preceding decades. Thus, it is very challenging to forecast future progress. It is possible that we have reached a plateau, and we wi…
